title: Balanço Hídrico Diário para Irrigação
text: Para calcular o balanço de água solo, o primeiro passo é obter dados meteorológicos diários. Para este exemplo didático, vamos simular 65 dias de dados de evapotranspiração de referência (ET0) e precipitação diretamente R.
code: library(irritool)  # Simulando 65 dias de dados climáticos para o ciclo da cultura dias_ciclo <- 65 set.seed(42)  # ET0 em mm/dia eto_sim <- round(runif(dias_ciclo, 3, 6), 1)  # Chuva em mm/dia (maior parte dos dias secos com alguns eventos isolados de chuva) chuva_sim <- round(sample(c(rep(0, 55), runif(10, 5, 30))), 0)

what: 2. Construindo a Curva do Coeficiente de Cultura (Kc)
title: Balanço Hídrico Diário para Irrigação
text: culturas consomem água em taxas diferentes dependendo da sua fase fenológica. função calc_kc_curve() utiliza metodologia da FAO-56 para construir série diária de Kc. Vamos simular um ciclo de 65 dias para uma hortaliça (ex: Alface), dividida em quatro fases: Inicial, Desenvolvimento da Cultura, Fase Intermediária e Fase Final.
code: # Definindo os parâmetros de Kc e a duração das fases (em dias) parametros_kc <- c(0.7, 1.0, 0.95) fases <- c(15, 20, 20, 10)  curva_alface <- calc_kc_curve(   kc_points = parametros_kc,   stage_lengths = fases,   crop = "Alface" )  # Visualizando o gráfico gerado automaticamente curva_alface$kc_plot

what: 3. Simulando o Balanço Hídrico no Solo
title: Balanço Hídrico Diário para Irrigação
text: Com os dados climáticos e os valores diários de Kc estabelecidos, podemos rodar simulação da umidade solo. função calc_water_balance() monitora Capacidade Total de Água Disponível (TAW), Água Facilmente Disponível (RAW) e depleção atual (déficit). Utilizaremos regra de irrigação por limite ("threshold"), que aplica água automaticamente sempre que depleção solo ultrapassar RAW, garantindo que planta não sofra estresse hídrico.
code: # Parâmetros do solo e sistema radicular profundidade_raiz <- 300  # mm umidade_cc <- 0.30        # Capacidade de campo (m3/m3) umidade_pmp <- 0.15       # Ponto de murcha permanente (m3/m3) fator_p <- 0.55           # Fator de depleção (p)  resultado_balanco <- calc_water_balance(   et0 = eto_sim,   rainfall = chuva_sim,   daily_kc_values = curva_alface$kc_serie,   root_depth = profundidade_raiz,   theta_fc = umidade_cc,   theta_wp = umidade_pmp,   depletion_factor = fator_p,   initial_depletion = 0, # Iniciando na capacidade de campo   irrigation_rule = "threshold" )

previous_headings: 3. Simulando o Balanço Hídrico no Solo
title: Balanço Hídrico Diário para Irrigação
text: função retorna tanto os dados diários detalhados quanto um resumo dos totais acumulados ao longo ciclo. O sumário mostra exatamente qual foi lâmina total de irrigação aplicada e quantos eventos de irrigação foram acionados para manter cultura fora da zona de estresse. Também podemos inspecionar o registro diário detalhado para analisar dinâmica da água dia dia:

title: Daily Soil Water Balance for Irrigation
text: calculate soil water balance, first step obtain daily meteorological data. educational example, simulate 65 days reference evapotranspiration (ET0) rainfall data directly R.
code: library(irritool)  # Simulating 65 days of climate data for the crop cycle cycle_days <- 65 set.seed(42)  # ET0 in mm/day eto_sim <- round(runif(cycle_days, 3, 6), 1)  # Rainfall in mm/day (mostly dry days with some isolated rain events) rain_sim <- round(sample(c(rep(0, 55), runif(10, 5, 30))), 0)

title: Daily Soil Water Balance for Irrigation
text: Crops consume water different rates depending phenological stage. calc_kc_curve() function uses FAO-56 methodology build daily Kc series. Let’s simulate 65-day vegetable crop cycle (e.g., Lettuce), divided four stages: Initial, Crop Development, Mid-Season, Late-Season.
code: # Defining Kc parameters and stage lengths (in days) kc_params <- c(0.7, 1.0, 0.95) stages <- c(15, 20, 20, 10)  lettuce_curve <- calc_kc_curve(   kc_points = kc_params,   stage_lengths = stages,   crop = "Lettuce" )  # Viewing the automatically generated plot lettuce_curve$kc_plot

title: Daily Soil Water Balance for Irrigation
text: climate data daily Kc values established, can run soil moisture simulation. calc_water_balance() function monitors Total Available Water (TAW), Readily Available Water (RAW), current depletion (deficit). use "threshold" irrigation rule, automatically applies water whenever soil depletion exceeds RAW, ensuring plant suffer water stress.
code: # Soil and root system parameters root_depth_val <- 300  # mm theta_fc_val <- 0.30   # Field capacity (m3/m3) theta_wp_val <- 0.15   # Wilting point (m3/m3) p_factor_val <- 0.55   # Depletion factor (p)  balance_results <- calc_water_balance(   et0 = eto_sim,   rainfall = rain_sim,   daily_kc_values = lettuce_curve$kc_serie,   root_depth = root_depth_val,   theta_fc = theta_fc_val,   theta_wp = theta_wp_val,   depletion_factor = p_factor_val,   initial_depletion = 0, # Starting at field capacity   irrigation_rule = "threshold" )

title: Daily Soil Water Balance for Irrigation
text: function returns detailed daily data summary accumulated totals cycle. summary shows exactly much total irrigation applied many irrigation events triggered keep crop stress zone. can also inspect detailed daily log analyze water dynamics day day:

title: Irrigation Tools in R
text: extract_brdwgd_point(): Extracts time series meteorological variables (precipitation, ETo, temperatures, radiation, etc.) specific coordinates BR-DWGD gridded database NetCDF files (Xavier et al.). calc_kc_curve(): Builds daily crop coefficient (Kc) time series based four development stages FAO-56 methodology, returning numeric values ready--use ggplot2 chart. calc_water_balance(): Calculates daily soil water balance crop, estimating actual evapotranspiration (ETc), water deficit, irrigation requirements, water surplus (deep percolation/runoff).

title: Irrigation Tools in R
text: basic workflow showing package functions integrate simulate crop cycle.
code: library(irritool)  # 1. Obtain climate data for a coordinate (requires local NetCDF files) # As this is a reproducible example, we leave the extraction commented out: # climate_data <- extract_brdwgd_point( #   target_longitude = -50.59,  #   target_latitude = -27.28,  #   weather_variables = c("pr", "ETo"), #   nc_files_directory = "path/to/files" # )  # For this example, we will use mock climate data: cycle_days <- 65 set.seed(123) rain_data <- sample(c(rep(0, 60), runif(5, 5, 25))) eto_data <- runif(cycle_days, 3, 5)  # 2. Generate the Kc curve for the crop (e.g., Lettuce) lettuce_kc <- calc_kc_curve(   kc_points = c(0.7, 1.0, 0.95),   stage_lengths = c(15, 20, 20, 10),   crop = "Lettuce" )  # View the generated plot print(lettuce_kc$kc_plot) # 3. Simulate the soil water balance water_balance <- calc_water_balance(   et0 = eto_data,   rainfall = rain_data,   daily_kc_values = lettuce_kc$kc_serie, # Consuming the output from the previous function   root_depth = 300,   theta_fc = 0.30,   theta_wp = 0.15,   depletion_factor = 0.55,   irrigation_rule = "threshold" )  # View the first few days of the soil water balance head(water_balance$water_balance_data) #>   day rainfall      et0 root_depth taw   raw depletion_start  kc ks      etc #> 1   1        0 4.564589        300  45 24.75        0.000000 0.7  1 3.195212 #> 2   2        0 3.187190        300  45 24.75        3.195212 0.7  1 2.231033 #> 3   3        0 3.933558        300  45 24.75        5.426245 0.7  1 2.753491 #> 4   4        0 4.023011        300  45 24.75        8.179736 0.7  1 2.816108 #> 5   5        0 4.199978        300  45 24.75       10.995843 0.7  1 2.939985 #> 6   6        0 3.665647        300  45 24.75       13.935828 0.7  1 2.565953 #>   depletion_end irrigation_applied water_surplus #> 1      3.195212                  0             0 #> 2      5.426245                  0             0 #> 3      8.179736                  0             0 #> 4     10.995843                  0             0 #> 5     13.935828                  0             0 #> 6     16.501781                  0             0  # View the summary depths (totals) print(water_balance$summary_depths) #> $total_rainfall #> [1] 91.16689 #>  #> $total_water_surplus #> [1] 35.75595 #>  #> $net_rainfall #> [1] 55.41094 #>  #> $total_etc #> [1] 228.1546 #>  #> $total_irrigation_applied #> [1] 158.6104 #>  #> $irrigation_events_count #> [1] 6

title: Irrigation Tools in R
text: Allen, R. G., Pereira, L. S., Raes, D., & Smith, M. (1998). Crop evapotranspiration - Guidelines computing crop water requirements. FAO Irrigation drainage paper 56. Xavier, . C., King, C. W., & Scanlon, B. R. (2016). Daily gridded meteorological variables Brazil (1980–2013). International Journal Climatology.

title: Generate Crop Coefficient (Kc) Curve — calc_kc_curve
text: Constructs time series daily crop coefficient (Kc) values generates ggplot2 visualization representing Kc curve crop cycle, following FAO-56 guidelines. curve divided four stages: initial, development, mid-season, late-season.

title: Generate Crop Coefficient (Kc) Curve — calc_kc_curve
text: kc_points Numeric vector length 3. Contains crop coefficients initial stage (Kc_ini), mid-season stage (Kc_mid), end late-season stage (Kc_end). stage_lengths Numeric vector length 4. duration (days) growth stage: initial, development, mid-season, late-season. crop Character (optional). Name crop, used customize plot title.

title: Generate Crop Coefficient (Kc) Curve — calc_kc_curve
text: list containing: kc_serie: numeric vector daily Kc values entire cycle. kc_plot: ggplot2 object displaying constructed curve key annotations. kc_data: data frame containing days corresponding Kc values.

title: Generate Crop Coefficient (Kc) Curve — calc_kc_curve
text: Allen, R. G., Pereira, L. S., Raes, D., & Smith, M. (1998). Crop evapotranspiration - Guidelines computing crop water requirements. FAO Irrigation drainage paper 56.

what: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
title: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
text: Calculates daily soil water balance crop specified period. function accounts reference evapotranspiration, rainfall, crop coefficients, soil properties, irrigation rules estimate actual crop evapotranspiration (ETc), soil water depletion, irrigation needs, water surplus.

title: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
code: calc_water_balance(   et0,   rainfall,   daily_kc_values,   root_depth,   theta_fc,   theta_wp,   depletion_factor,   initial_depletion = 0,   day_numbers = NULL,   irrigation_rule = "threshold" )

title: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
text: et0 Numeric vector. Daily reference evapotranspiration (mm/day). rainfall Numeric vector. Daily rainfall (mm/day). daily_kc_values Numeric vector. Daily crop coefficient (Kc) values. root_depth Numeric vector single value. Daily root depth (mm). single value provided, applies days. theta_fc Numeric. Volumetric soil water content field capacity (m³/m³). theta_wp Numeric. Volumetric soil water content wilting point (m³/m³). depletion_factor Numeric. fraction Total Available Water (TAW) crop can extract root zone without suffering water stress ('p' factor FAO-56). Must 0 1. initial_depletion Numeric. Initial soil water depletion start day 1 (mm). Defaults 0 (field capacity). day_numbers Numeric vector (optional). Sequence days (e.g., day year). Defaults sequence 1 length daily_kc_values. irrigation_rule Character Integer. Defines irrigation trigger. Use "threshold" (default) irrigate depletion exceeds Readily Available Water (RAW), positive integer N irrigate fixed N-day interval.

title: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
text: list containing: water_balance_data data.frame detailed daily soil water balance components: day: Day number. rainfall: Input rainfall (mm). et0: Input reference ET0 (mm). root_depth: Root depth day (mm). taw: Total Available Water root zone (mm). raw: Readily Available Water root zone (mm). depletion_start: Soil water depletion start day (mm). kc: Crop coefficient day. ks: Water stress coefficient (0-1). etc: Actual crop evapotranspiration (mm). depletion_end: Soil water depletion end day irrigation (mm). irrigation_applied: Irrigation depth applied (mm). water_surplus: Water surplus (deep percolation runoff) (mm). summary_depths list cumulative water balance components: total_rainfall: Total input rainfall (mm). total_water_surplus: Total water surplus (mm). net_rainfall: Total rainfall minus total water surplus (mm). total_etc: Total actual crop evapotranspiration (mm). total_irrigation_applied: Total irrigation depth applied (mm). irrigation_events_count: Number irrigation events.

title: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
text: Allen, R. G., Pereira, L. S., Raes, D., & Smith, M. (1998). Crop evapotranspiration - Guidelines computing crop water requirements. FAO Irrigation drainage paper 56.

title: Calculate Daily Soil Water Balance — calc_water_balance
code: # For a standalone example, let's create a dummy daily_kc_values daily_kc_lettuce <- c(   rep(0.7, 15),   seq(0.7, 1.05, length.out = 20),   rep(1.05, 20),   seq(1.05, 0.9, length.out = 10) ) # Total 65 days  set.seed(123) # for reproducibility sim_days <- length(daily_kc_lettuce) et0_data <- round(runif(sim_days, 2, 5), 1) rain_data <- round(sample(c(rep(0, sim_days - 5), runif(5, 3, 30))), 0)  root_depth_val <- 300 # mm p_factor_val <- 0.55 theta_fc_val <- 0.30 # m3/m3 theta_wp_val <- 0.15 # m3/m3  water_balance_results <- calc_water_balance(   et0 = et0_data,   rainfall = rain_data,   daily_kc_values = daily_kc_lettuce,   root_depth = root_depth_val,   theta_fc = theta_fc_val,   theta_wp = theta_wp_val,   depletion_factor = p_factor_val,   initial_depletion = 0, # Starting at field capacity   irrigation_rule = "threshold" )  print(head(water_balance_results$water_balance_data)) #>   day rainfall et0 root_depth taw   raw depletion_start  kc ks  etc #> 1   1        0 2.9        300  45 24.75            0.00 0.7  1 2.03 #> 2   2        0 4.4        300  45 24.75            2.03 0.7  1 3.08 #> 3   3        0 3.2        300  45 24.75            5.11 0.7  1 2.24 #> 4   4        0 4.6        300  45 24.75            7.35 0.7  1 3.22 #> 5   5        0 4.8        300  45 24.75           10.57 0.7  1 3.36 #> 6   6        0 2.1        300  45 24.75           13.93 0.7  1 1.47 #>   depletion_end irrigation_applied water_surplus #> 1          2.03                  0             0 #> 2          5.11                  0             0 #> 3          7.35                  0             0 #> 4         10.57                  0             0 #> 5         13.93                  0             0 #> 6         15.40                  0             0 print(water_balance_results$summary_depths) #> $total_rainfall #> [1] 104 #>  #> $total_water_surplus #> [1] 58.67 #>  #> $net_rainfall #> [1] 45.33 #>  #> $total_etc #> [1] 203.7632 #>  #> $total_irrigation_applied #> [1] 158.4332 #>  #> $irrigation_events_count #> [1] 6 #>   # Example with fixed interval irrigation (every 7 days) water_balance_fixed_interval <- calc_water_balance(   et0 = et0_data,   rainfall = rain_data,   daily_kc_values = daily_kc_lettuce,   root_depth = root_depth_val,   theta_fc = theta_fc_val,   theta_wp = theta_wp_val,   depletion_factor = p_factor_val,   irrigation_rule = 7 ) print(water_balance_fixed_interval$summary_depths$total_irrigation_applied) #> [1] 142.4415 print(water_balance_fixed_interval$summary_depths$irrigation_events_count) #> [1] 8

what: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
title: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
text: Extracts daily time series specified weather variables given longitude latitude Brazilian Daily Weather Gridded Data (BR-DWGD) NetCDF files. function identifies closest grid cell target coordinates.

title: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
code: get_brdwgd_weather(   target_longitude,   target_latitude,   weather_variables = c("pr", "Tmin", "Tmax", "Rs", "RH", "u2", "ETo"),   date_range = NULL,   nc_files_directory = "." )

title: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
text: target_longitude Numeric. Longitude target location decimal degrees. target_latitude Numeric. Latitude target location decimal degrees. weather_variables Character vector. Variables extract. Allowed values : "pr" (precipitation), "Tmin", "Tmax", "Rs" (solar radiation), "RH" (relative humidity), "u2" (wind speed), "ETo". Defaults . date_range Character vector length 2 ("YYYY-MM-DD"). start end dates. NULL (default), extracts entire available period. nc_files_directory Character. Path local directory containing BR-DWGD NetCDF files. Defaults current working directory ".".

title: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
text: tibble containing date, actual lat lon closest grid cell, columns requested weather variable.

title: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
text: Xavier, . C., King, C. W., & Scanlon, B. R. (2016). Daily gridded meteorological variables Brazil (1980–2013). International Journal Climatology. https://sites.google.com/site/alexandrecandidoxavierufes/brazilian-daily-weather-gridded-data

title: Extract Point Data from BR-DWGD — get_brdwgd_weather
code: target_longitude <- -50.5995 target_latitude <- -27.2863 weather_variables <- c("pr", "ETo", "Tmax") date_range <- c("1983-01-01", "1983-03-31") nc_files_directory <- "D:/clima_Xavier"  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Ensure the path to your NetCDF files is correct weather_data_point <- get_brdwgd_weather(   target_longitude = -50.5995,   target_latitude = -27.2863,   weather_variables = c("pr", "ETo", "Tmax"),   date_range = c("1983-01-01", "1983-03-31"),   nc_files_directory = "D:/clima_Xavier" ) print(head(weather_data_point)) } # }
